锄.origin: The metal radical 钅 shows it's a tool, paired with 助 (to help) — a hoe helps you work the soil.

sentence.en: Grandpa uses a hoe to grow vegetables.

粒.origin: The rice radical 米 next to 立 (to stand) — picture one tiny grain of rice standing all by itself.

sentence.en: There is a grain of rice on the table.

皆.origin: Picture people side by side 比 above the sun 白 — when everyone shines together, it means all.

sentence.en: We are all good friends.

辛.origin: A pointed shape standing tall — it once meant a sharp tool, and now it means a spicy, hard, bitter feeling.

sentence.en: Mom works very hard.

苦.origin: The grass radical 艹 over 古 (old) — like an old bitter herb that makes your tongue scrunch up.

sentence.en: This medicine is very bitter.

洁.origin: Water 氵 beside 吉 (lucky) — fresh water washes everything clean and pure.

sentence.en: Her room is very tidy.

牌.origin: Made from a flat slice of wood 片 — a little board you hold, like a playing card or a sign.

sentence.en: Let's play cards together.

报.origin: A hand 扌 reaching out to pass on news — 报 means to tell, announce, or a newspaper.

sentence.en: Dad reads the newspaper every day.

半.origin: Two little strokes 丷 splitting something down the middle — that line cuts it into two halves.

sentence.en: The apple is split into two halves.

剩.origin: A knife 刂 stands beside 乘 — after cutting and taking, what is left over is the leftovers.

sentence.en: A little rice is left in the bowl.

夫.origin: A grown person 大 with a hairpin 一 across the top — a man old enough to be a husband.

sentence.en: That farmer is very busy.

鸦.origin: The bird radical 鸟 with 牙 for sound — a big black bird that goes 'caw,' the crow.

sentence.en: There is a crow in the tree.

泥.origin: Water 氵 mixed into soil — add a splash of water to dirt and you get squishy mud.

sentence.en: After the rain there is mud on the road.

肥.origin: The flesh radical ⺼ shows it's about the body — 肥 means plump, round, and fat.

sentence.en: This cat is fat and cute.

料.origin: Rice 米 beside a measuring scoop 斗 — measuring out grain, like gathering ingredients and materials.

sentence.en: We have prepared the materials.

言.origin: Picture a tongue lifting out of a mouth 口 — 言 means words and speech.

sentence.en: He spoke a few words.

丰.origin: Three lines crossed by one stalk — like a plant heavy with leaves, full and bountiful.

sentence.en: This year's harvest is bountiful.

富.origin: A roof 宀 over a full storehouse 畐 — a house packed with good things means rich.

sentence.en: This country is very wealthy.

晴.origin: The sun 日 next to 青 (blue) — bright sun in a blue sky means clear, sunny weather.

sentence.en: Today is a bright sunny day.

蚂.origin: The insect radical 虫 with 马 for sound — a tiny crawling bug, the busy little ant.

sentence.en: There are many ants on the ground.

蚁.origin: The insect radical 虫 beside 义 — another part of the word for the tiny, hard-working ant.

sentence.en: The little ant carried away the bread crumb.

搬.origin: A hand 扌 doing the work 般 — 搬 means to use your hands to move and carry things.

sentence.en: We are moving house next week.

伞.origin: Look at the shape — a round top with a handle below, just like an open umbrella keeping you dry.

sentence.en: It's raining, open the umbrella quickly.

察.origin: Under a roof 宀 you look closely at things 祭 — 察 means to examine and observe carefully.

sentence.en: We watch the little fish closely.

懂.origin: The heart radical 忄 beside 董 — when your heart truly gets it, you understand.

sentence.en: I understand what you mean.

考.origin: Shaped like the 'old' radical 耂 — wise elders give a test, so 考 means to examine.

sentence.en: We have an exam tomorrow.

探.origin: A hand 扌 reaching deep into something — 探 means to reach in, search, and explore.

sentence.en: They go exploring in the mountains.

员.origin: A mouth 口 above a shell of money 贝 — picture a worker or member earning their pay.

sentence.en: He is an athlete.

次.origin: Two ice strokes 冫 beside a yawning person 欠 — yawn after yawn, one time after another.

sentence.en: This is my first time on a boat.

类.origin: Grains of rice 米 over 大 (big) — sort the big pile of rice into groups, into kinds.

sentence.en: There are many kinds of fruit.

实.origin: A roof 宀 over 头 — what is really inside the house is solid, true, and real.

sentence.en: What he said is very true.

梦.origin: Two trees 林 with the evening 夕 below — eyes shut in the dark of night brings a dream.

sentence.en: I had a dream last night.

植.origin: The tree radical 木 beside 直 (straight) — push a seed straight down into the soil to plant it.

sentence.en: I plant trees in the garden.

式.origin: A tool 弋 over a craftsman's square 工 — making things by a set pattern or style.

sentence.en: This is a new style.

程.origin: The grain radical 禾 beside 呈 — measuring how grain grows step by step, like a process.

sentence.en: The way to school is short.

器.origin: Four mouths 口 around a dog 犬 — picture pots and bowls guarded by a watchful dog.

sentence.en: This machine is very useful.

背.origin: Two people back to back 北 above the flesh radical ⺼ — that's your body's back.

sentence.en: He carries a schoolbag on his back.

陆.origin: The mound radical 阝 stands for hills and earth — 陆 means solid land and the great continent.

sentence.en: The plane lands on the ground.

超.origin: The 'run' radical 走 with 召 for sound — run so fast you leap past everyone, you go beyond.

sentence.en: Mom goes to the supermarket to buy food.

周.origin: A border 冂 wrapped around the land — going all the way around means a circle, or a week.

sentence.en: This weekend we are going to the park.
